faq.q: What bitrate should I use for 1080p video?
faq.a: For 1080p, typical bitrates range from 5-10 Mbps for streaming to 15-25 Mbps for high-quality downloads.

faq.q: How much storage do I need for a 2-hour 4K movie?
faq.a: A 2-hour 4K movie at 25 Mbps requires approximately 22.5 GB (using H.264). With H.265 compression, expect about 11 GB.

faq.q: What's the difference between H.264 and H.265 codec?
faq.a: H.265 (HEVC) offers roughly 50% better compression than H.264, allowing the same quality at half the bitrate or file size.

faq.q: How much does audio bitrate add to video?
faq.a: Audio typically adds 1-5 Mbps to your total bitrate, depending on quality and channel count.

faq.q: What bitrate does Netflix use for 4K streaming?
faq.a: Netflix streams 4K content at approximately 15-25 Mbps using H.265, and 1080p at 5-7 Mbps.

faq.q: Do different platforms require different bitrates?
faq.a: Yes. YouTube recommends 4-10 Mbps for 1080p, TikTok ~6 Mbps, Instagram ~8 Mbps. Each platform has optimal settings.

faq.q: What is RAW video and how much storage does it need?
faq.a: RAW video stores uncompressed data directly from the camera sensor, requiring 100+ Mbps and massive storage—multiple GB per minute.
